summary:
Both zinc oxide eugeneol cement and polycarboxylate cement use chelation involving polyacrilic acid binding with calcium ions from dentin for adhesive action. This chelation process enables their ability to bond effectively to dental tissues through an ion-exchange mechanism.  

Final Answer: C

The summary should not contain any extra information that was not present in the original text.

Both zinc oxide-eugenol and polycarboxylate cements achieve adherence primarily through chelation—specifically, interactions where polyacrylic acid binds calcium ions in dentin. This ion-chelating property underlies their effective bonding capability within dental restorations.
